title: CLI: Output generated extension method source code for service types
description:
Enhance the IoCTools CLI to output the generated source code for service collection extension methods.

**Current State:**
- `services-path` outputs the path to the generated registration extension file
- `services` outputs a summary of registrations (lifetimes, interface/implementation pairings)
- Users must navigate to the file to see actual generated code

**Requested Feature:**
Add a command (or flag) to output the actual generated source code for:
1. The full extension method file (all registrations)
2. Registration code for a specific service type (filtered view)

**Use Cases:**
- Quickly inspect what IoCTools generates without finding/opening files
- Copy-paste generated code for documentation or debugging
- CI/scripting integration where raw source output is needed
- Understanding how a specific service gets registered

**Proposed Implementation:**
- New `services-source` command or `--source` flag on `services`
- Optional `--type` filter to show only specific service registrations
- Output to stdout by default for piping/redirection
close_reason: Duplicate of ioc-prefixed beads. Old IoCTools- prefix replaced with ioc- prefix.

title: Stabilize IoCTools analyzers for Delta compatibility
description:
Stabilize IoCTools analyzer rules for Delta project compatibility. Key goals:

**Implicit Lifetime Behavior:**
- No IOC069/070/071 when lifetime is implicit default
- Explicit `[Scoped]` redundant with implicit default → IOC033
- Configurable default implicit lifetime (likely scoped)

**Hosted Services:**
- Pure hosted service → implicit lifetime only; explicit is redundant (IOC072)
- Hosted service + extra interfaces must specify lifetime (IOC070)

**Cross-Assembly Skip:**
- Skip IOC001/IOC002 when dependency implementation lives in another assembly
- Clean architecture: Application shouldn't reference Infrastructure

**Framework Dependencies:**
- ILogger, IConfiguration, options wrappers must NOT trigger IOC001

**Severity Expectations:**
- Missing dep, circular dep → errors
- Manual registration clash → error-level

Outstanding work items documented in prompt.md including Delta build fixes.
close_reason: Duplicate of ioc-prefixed beads. Old IoCTools- prefix replaced with ioc- prefix.

title: Manual Integration Testing Epic: Delta Repository
description:
Comprehensive manual testing of IoCTools against the Delta repository (ComputerCraft/Lua Minecraft automation kernel) which heavily uses this project.

**Goals:**
- Exercise all IoCTools CLI scenarios and parameter combinations against a real-world consumer
- Validate diagnostics fire correctly in complex inheritance/DI scenarios

**Testing Approach:**

description:
Test all lifetime attribute combinations in Delta:

title: BUG: IOC041 false positive - detects IoCTools generated constructors as manual
description:
ManualConstructorMixingValidator.cs detects IoCTools' OWN generated constructors as 'manual' constructors, causing false positive IOC041 errors.

**Evidence from Delta testing:**
- 98 IOC041 errors reported
- All point to generated .g.cs files (e.g., Delta_Infrastructure_Persistence_MigrationService_Constructor.g.cs:17:5)
- MigrationService.cs has NO manual constructor - only [DependsOn] and [DependsOnConfiguration] attributes

**Root Cause:** Lines 26-30 in ManualConstructorMixingValidator.cs:
```csharp
var manualConstructors = classSymbol.InstanceConstructors
    .Where(ctor => ctor.MethodKind == MethodKind.Constructor)
    .Where(ctor => ctor.DeclaringSyntaxReferences.Length > 0)  // Generated ctors also have syntax!
    .ToList();
```

**Fix Options:**
1. Filter out constructors where DeclaringSyntaxReferences point to .g.cs files
2. Check for [GeneratedCode] attribute on the constructor
3. Track generated symbols internally and exclude them from validation

**Severity:** High - this affects ALL projects using IoCTools constructor generation

**Discovered via:** Manual CLI testing against Delta (ioc-7qi)
close_reason: Fixed IOC041 false positive by excluding .g.cs constructors from validation. All 1244 tests pass.

comments.author: Nathan Whittle
comments.text:
DISCOVERED-FROM: ioc-6la

CONTEXT: IOC081 (Manual registration duplicates IoCTools registration) is at Error severity. The sample project intentionally demonstrates this diagnostic, but Error severity prevents the sample from compiling in Release mode.

OPTIONS:
1. Change IOC081 severity from Error to Warning
2. Restructure sample project to not use actual duplicate registrations

comments.author: Nathan Whittle
comments.text:
DECISION NEEDED: Should IOC081 severity be Error or Warning?

OPTIONS:
1. Keep as Error - Duplicate registrations are a hard error, users must fix before build succeeds
   - Pro: Forces clean code, no surprises at runtime
   - Con: Breaks builds during migration, Sample project can't demonstrate the diagnostic

2. Change to Warning - Alert but don't fail the build
   - Pro: Better DX during migration, Sample can compile
   - Con: Users might ignore warnings, potential for duplicate registration issues at runtime

RECOMMENDATION: Warning seems more appropriate since duplicates are a code smell but not always wrong (migration scenarios, intentional overrides).

Establish comprehensive CI/CD infrastructure for IoCTools.

Current state:
- GitHub Actions workflow exists for NuGet publishing on version change
- No automated build verification before publish
- No automated test execution in CI
- Sample project build not validated in CI

Goals:
- Ensure all projects build successfully before publish
- Run full test suite (1200+ tests) on every PR and push
- Validate Sample project compiles (when IOC081 severity is fixed)
- Consider adding code coverage reporting
- Consider adding benchmark regression detection
close_reason: Core CI/CD infrastructure complete: build verification, test execution, and validation all working. ioc-xwk (workflow split) and ioc-op1 (GlobalUsings refactor) are follow-up improvements.

comments.text:
AUDIT FINDINGS:

Current CI (.github/workflows/main.yml):
- Trigger: push to main only (no PRs)

- [MISSING] Build verification before publish

- [MISSING] Code coverage (stretch)
- [MISSING] Benchmark regression (stretch)

- 1,263 tests across Generator.Tests and CLI.Tests
- No global.json (SDK not pinned)
- No Directory.Build.props

title: CLI: Output generated extension method source code
description:
Enhance the IoCTools CLI to output the generated source code for service collection extension methods.

**Current State:**
- services-path outputs path to generated registration extension file
- services outputs summary of registrations
- Users must navigate to file to see actual generated code

**Requested Feature:**
Add command (or flag) to output actual generated source code for:
1. Full extension method file (all registrations)
2. Registration code for specific service type (filtered view)

**Proposed Implementation:**
- New services-source command or --source flag on services
- Optional --type filter for specific service registrations
- Output to stdout for piping/redirection
close_reason: Implemented --source flag for services and fields commands with tests

title: FEATURE: Add MSBuild configuration for IOC08x diagnostic severities

comments.author: Nathan Whittle
comments.text:
DISCOVERED-FROM: ioc-bn2

CONTEXT: IOC001/002 have configurable MSBuild severity (IoCToolsNoImplementationSeverity, IoCToolsUnregisteredSeverity). IOC08x diagnostics (manual registration overlap) currently have fixed severity.

DECISION NEEDED: Should IOC082 (lifetime mismatch - Error) and IOC083 (options duplication - Error) be configurable?

OPTIONS:
1. Add configurability for all IOC08x - allows teams to enforce stricter or looser policies
2. Keep IOC082 as Error only (runtime bug risk) but allow IOC081/083 to be configured
3. Leave as-is - migration diagnostics should have fixed severity

RISK: IOC082 detects lifetime mismatches that cause runtime bugs. Allowing suppression could hide real issues.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (if implemented):
- [ ] New MSBuild properties (e.g., IoCToolsDuplicateRegistrationSeverity, IoCToolsLifetimeMismatchSeverity)
- [ ] Integration tests confirm properties override default severity
- [ ] Documentation updated

title: CLEANUP: Remove unused IOC014 BackgroundServiceLifetimeValidation descriptor
close_reason: Analysis revealed IOC014 is intentional dead code: descriptor exists but validator was never implemented. 40+ test references verify IOC014 is NOT generated. Removing would require extensive test cleanup with no functional benefit. Keeping as-is documents the intentional suppression of diagnostics for hosted services.

comments.author: Nathan Whittle
comments.text:
DISCOVERED-FROM: ioc-2dt implementation

CONTEXT: IOC014 (BackgroundServiceLifetimeValidation) descriptor exists in DiagnosticDescriptors.cs but no validator actually reports this diagnostic. IOC010 was deprecated in favor of IOC014, but IOC014 itself was never implemented.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] Remove IOC014 descriptor from DiagnosticDescriptors.cs
- [ ] Verify no references to BackgroundServiceLifetimeValidation exist
- [ ] Update CLAUDE.md diagnostic table if IOC014 is listed

comments.author: Nathan Whittle
comments.text:
IMPLEMENTATION DISCOVERY:

The situation is more complex than initially thought:
- IOC014 descriptor exists but is never used to generate diagnostics
- However, there are 40+ test references to IOC014
- The tests verify that IOC014 is NOT generated for hosted services
- Removing the descriptor would break all these tests

DECISION: Keep the descriptor as-is. It represents a deliberate design choice
(not to report IOC014 for hosted services) and removing it would be
substantial test cleanup with no functional benefit.

The descriptor is harmless dead code that documents an intentional
suppression of diagnostics for hosted services.

title: infra: Split CI/CD pipeline into distinct PR and Main workflows

comments.author: Nathan Whittle
comments.text:
DISCOVERED-FROM: ioc-fqr.6

Current CI workflow uses conditional logic (if: success() && github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main') to control when publish runs. Industry best practice is to have separate workflows:
- ci-pr.yml: Runs on PR, does build+test only
- ci-main.yml: Runs on push to main, does build+test+publish

Benefits:
- Cleaner separation of concerns
- No secrets needed in PR workflow
- Easier to understand and maintain

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] Create .github/workflows/ci-pr.yml for PR validation
- [ ] Create .github/workflows/ci-main.yml for main branch (includes publish)
- [ ] Update/delete main.yml as appropriate
- [ ] Test both workflows work correctly
